title: CMMC Compliance Assessment

index=cui sourcetype="o365:audit" Workload=SharePoint
| search CUI_label="true" AND SharingScope="Anyone"
| stats count by UserId, SiteUrl, ObjectId
| sort -count
description: Tracks DFARS 7012 logging, FCI/CUI handling, and practice maturity for CMMC L2/L3.
value: Tracks DFARS 7012 logging, FCI/CUI handling, and practice maturity for CMMC L2/L3.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Microsoft 365 GCC High, endpoint DLP.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="o365:audit"`, `sourcetype="dlp:cui"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Encrypt CUI at rest; block personal email domains; SSP alignment to CMMC domains.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cui sourcetype="o365:audit" Workload=SharePoint
| search CUI_label="true" AND SharingScope="Anyone"
| stats count by UserId, SiteUrl, ObjectId
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**CMMC Compliance Assessment** — Tracks DFARS 7012 logging, FCI/CUI handling, and practice maturity for CMMC L2/L3.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="o365:audit"`, `sourcetype="dlp:cui"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Microsoft 365 GCC High, endpoint DLP. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cui; **sourcetype**: o365:audit. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cui, sourcetype="o365:audit". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by UserId, SiteUrl, ObjectId** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (policy violations), Bar chart (practices), Heatmap (domain × status).
grandmaExplanation: In our line of business, we pay close attention to "CMMC Compliance Assessment" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
